summary: Mobile app for physical verification of inventory assets across multiple locations. Features GPS capture, mandatory photo upload, asset entry form, auto date/time stamp, Google Sheet backend, admin dashboard view, and offline capability. Built for audit/physical verification purposes.
architectural_notes: Google Sheets used as backend via Google Sheets API or Apps Script
Offline-first architecture required with local storage and background sync
Mobile app must capture GPS coordinates and photos per asset entry
known_issues: GPS capture not working for new asset entries in Version 4
Camera button not working in Version 5 (photo capture broken)

existing_features.title: Create an asset entry form that captures: Asset ID/Name, Asset Category, Location (selectable from a list of multiple locations), Condition (Good/Fair/Poor/Missing), and free-text Remarks/Notes fields.
Automatically capture and attach the current date and time to every asset submission upon form completion. Display the timestamp prominently on each record.

Implement mandatory photo upload for each asset entry. The user must attach at least one photo before the form can be submitted. Support capturing a photo via device camera or uploading from the gallery.
Capture the device's current GPS coordinates (latitude and longitude) when the user submits an asset entry form and attach the location data to the record. Display coordinates on the submission.

Build an admin dashboard that displays all asset verification submissions in a tabular view. Support filtering by location, date range, and condition. Allow drilling into a single record to view full details including the photo and GPS coordinates.

Implement offline capability so auditors can fill out and queue asset entry forms without an internet connection. When connectivity is restored, queued submissions are automatically synced to the backend.

Design a mobile-first visual theme for the app: industrial/field-audit aesthetic with a clean, high-contrast layout using deep slate and amber accent colors, bold typography, large touch targets, and card-based layouts optimized for outdoor/bright-light use on mobile devices.

Display a dismissible 'Add to Home Screen' banner or modal prompt to mobile users when they first open the app, guiding them to install it as a PWA. The prompt should appear automatically on first visit on a mobile browser, include step-by-step instructions for both Android (Chrome) and iOS (Safari), and not appear again after the user dismisses it (persist dismissal in localStorage).

Replace the predefined location dropdown/select in the asset entry form with a blank free-text input field, allowing users to manually type any location value. The field should start empty with no pre-selected or default value. Update the location filter in the admin dashboard to also accept free-text input to match manually entered location values.

Add an 'Export to CSV' button in the Admin Dashboard page. When clicked, it fetches all currently filtered asset records and downloads them as a CSV file that is directly openable in Google Sheets. The CSV must include columns: Asset ID, Asset Name, Category, Location, Condition, Remarks, GPS Latitude, GPS Longitude, Photo Reference, and Timestamp. The downloaded filename should follow the pattern 'asset-records-YYYY-MM-DD.csv'.

Fix the GPS capture bug in the asset entry form (AssetEntryForm.tsx) so that latitude, longitude, and accuracy are correctly captured and attached to new asset submissions. The useGeolocation hook's `capture` callback must be invoked at the right lifecycle point, its state must be read after the async capture resolves, and the resulting coordinates must be included in the submission payload sent to the backend.

Add a delete asset capability to the frontend. In the Admin Dashboard (AdminDashboard.tsx), add a 'Delete' action for each asset record — accessible from the asset detail drawer and/or the asset table row. Clicking Delete shows a confirmation dialog before calling the backend deleteAsset function. On success, the asset list is refreshed and a success toast is shown. On failure, an error toast is shown.

Fix the camera/photo capture button in AssetEntryForm.tsx so that tapping 'Take Photo' or 'Upload from Gallery' correctly opens the device camera or file picker on mobile devices. The PhotoUpload component's file input must have the correct `accept` and `capture` attributes, and any event handler wiring issues preventing the button from triggering the input must be resolved.

Fix the GPS capture flow in AssetEntryForm.tsx so that GPS coordinates are reliably obtained on form submission. The `useGeolocation` hook's `capture` function must be called and awaited before building the submission payload, and the resolved latitude, longitude, and accuracy values must be read from the promise result (not from stale React state) and correctly included in the asset record sent to the backend.

new_feature_requests.summary: Mandatory photo upload for each asset entry
Asset entry form for recording inventory details
Auto date/time stamp on each submission
Google Sheet as backend data store
Admin dashboard view to review submissions
Offline capability with sync when back online
Multi-location support for asset verification across sites

Store all asset verification submissions persistently in the backend. Each record must include: Asset ID, Asset Name, Category, Location, Condition, Remarks, GPS coordinates, photo reference, and timestamp.
Add 'Add to Home Screen' prompt/guidance for mobile users to install the app as a PWA
Location dropdown should be blank by default, allowing users to manually type/add a location instead of selecting from a predefined list
Add a section/feature to download asset records to Google Sheet (export functionality)

Add a backend query function that returns all asset verification records as a structured list suitable for export, including all fields: Asset ID, Asset Name, Category, Location, Condition, Remarks, GPS coordinates (latitude and longitude), photo reference, and timestamp.
Add delete asset functionality to remove asset records

Add a `deleteAsset` function to the backend Motoko actor (backend/main.mo) that accepts an asset record ID and removes the corresponding entry from the in-memory map. The function must return a success/failure variant indicating whether the record was found and deleted.
